steps.title: Identify the goal
steps.content: The student wants to specify how many chromosomes the pineapple has — a precise number, not a comparison or a description of what chromosomes do.

steps.title: Test each choice against the goal
steps.content:
• Choice A — The pineapple's chromosomes contain genes, which are critical to determining an organism's physical traits.: Describes what chromosomes do, not how many the pineapple has. ✗
  • Choice B — The pineapple (Ananas comosus) has fifty chromosomes.: Directly states the exact chromosome count: fifty. This is precisely what the goal requires. ✓
  • Choice C — The pineapple (Ananas comosus) and the melon (Cucumis melo) both have chromosomes, but the pineapple has more than the melon does.: Gives a relative comparison, not an exact number. ✗
  • Choice D — The melon, a species of fruit, has twenty-four structures called chromosomes.: Specifies the melon's count, not the pineapple's. ✗
That matches Choice B — The pineapple (Ananas comosus) has fifty chromosomes..
